title: ranunculus hederaceus subsp. lenormandii
intro: Ranunculus hederaceus subsp. lenormandii, also known as Creeping Buttercup, is a perennial plant species in the ranunculaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and creeping habit. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus hederaceus subsp. lenormandii include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
